senses.definition: a series of lessons or lectures on a particular subject

examples.x: He completed a photography course to enhance his camera skills.
I'm taking an online course to learn a new language.
The company provided a training course for all new employees.
The online course includes video lectures, quizzes, and assignments.
The university offers a course in computer programming for beginners.

senses.definition: a part of a book that is intended to be used for learning a specific subject

examples.x: In the art lesson, we practiced drawing landscapes.
She studied the math lesson carefully to understand the concept.
The textbook has a vocabulary lesson with new words to learn.
We covered an interesting grammar lesson in our English class.

senses.definition: a branch or an area of knowledge that we study at a school, college, or university

examples.x: At university, Jane specialized in the subject of linguistics, studying various languages and their structures.
In elementary school, students learn about different subjects like mathematics, science, and history.
In high school, I found chemistry to be a challenging subject, but I persevered and improved my grades.
Mary's favorite subject in college is psychology because she enjoys understanding human behavior.
Physics is a fascinating subject that explains the fundamental laws of nature and the behavior of matter and energy.

senses.definition: a person's performance or capability in comparison to others

examples.x: At her current level of English, Sarah can have basic conversations and understand everyday expressions.
John is studying hard to reach a higher level of proficiency in playing the piano.
The beginners' Spanish course is designed for students at the A2 level.
The language school offers different levels of difficulty to cater to students' individual abilities.
The online course is suitable for learners at all levels, from beginners to advanced.

senses.definition: each of the two periods into which a year at schools or universities is divided

examples.x: At the end of each semester, students receive a report card with their grades.
During the semester break, many students travel to visit their families.
In the first semester, we covered basic grammar and vocabulary in the language course.
My favorite time of the semester is when we have group discussions and debates in our literature class.
This semester, I am taking classes in English, math, and history.

senses.definition: the main subject or course that a student studies at a university or college

examples.x: He chose biology as his major with the goal of pursuing a career in medicine.
Her major is biology, and she plans to pursue a career in research.

senses.definition: someone who is or was in the same class as you at school or college

examples.x: At the class reunion, old classmates reminisced about their shared experiences and achievements.
She reached out to her former classmates on social media to reconnect and catch up.
She became close friends with her classmate after working on a group project together.
He often studied with his classmates in the library during exam week.
The teacher encouraged collaboration among classmates to foster a supportive learning community.

senses.definition: a person we do a particular activity with, such as playing a game

examples.x: Alex's tennis partner is really skilled, and they make a great team.
In the cooking class, each student has a different partner every week.
Mark and Lisa are practice partners for learning a new musical instrument.
Paul and Sarah decided to become workout partners at the gym to motivate each other.
Sarah found a dance partner to participate in the upcoming competition.

senses.definition: a book or electronic resource that gives a list of words in alphabetical order and explains their meanings, or gives the equivalent words in a different language

examples.x: A pocket-sized dictionary can be handy during travels to help communicate in different languages.
Teachers often encourage students to expand their vocabulary using a thesaurus alongside a dictionary.
The online dictionary provides pronunciation guides for difficult words.

senses.definition: a way of testing how much someone knows about a subject

examples.x: In the language exam, we had to write a short essay on our favorite book.
Students are not allowed to use their phones during the exam.
The biology exam covers topics like genetics, cells, and ecology.
The students received their exam results and were happy to see their improvements.

senses.definition: an examination that consists of a set of questions, exercises, or activities to measure someone’s knowledge, skill, or ability

examples.x: After the language test, the teacher provided feedback to help the students improve their language skills.
Mark received a high score on the history test because he reviewed the material thoroughly.
The driving test includes both a written exam and a practical evaluation.
The math test had several challenging questions about algebra and geometry.

senses.definition: the act of repeatedly doing something to become better at doing it

examples.x: Daily practice of yoga can improve flexibility and reduce stress.
Joining a debate club provides ample opportunities for public speaking practice.
Language practice through conversation partners can enhance your fluency.
To become a better swimmer, consistent practice is essential.

senses.definition: something that a person spends time doing, particularly to accomplish a certain purpose

examples.x: Playing board games with family is an entertaining activity for the weekends.
Reading books is an enjoyable activity that helps expand your knowledge.
Solving puzzles and brain teasers can be a challenging but stimulating activity.

senses.definition: a question that can be answered by mathematics or logical thinking

examples.x: In the competition, each team had to solve the same set of problems
Our teacher gave us a problem to solve for homework.
She enjoys problems that involve patterns and sequences.
The crossword puzzle was a fun problem to solve over breakfast.
This math problem seems hard, but I think I can solve it.

senses.definition: to find an answer or solution to a question or problem

examples.x: A positive attitude can often help you solve various challenges in life.
She solved the problem by breaking it down into smaller steps.
The scientist conducted experiments to solve the scientific question.
The team collaborated to solve the technical issues with the website.
Can you solve this riddle before the time runs out?

senses.definition: a letter or number given by a teacher to show how a student is performing in class, school, etc.

examples.x: I received an A grade on my English test because I studied hard.
In the grading system, an A+ is the highest grade a student can achieve.
Mark's grades have improved significantly since he started studying regularly.
Sarah was disappointed with her grade on the science project but vowed to do better next time.
The students eagerly awaited their report cards to see their final grades.

senses.definition: a particular task involving careful study of a subject, done by school or college students

examples.x: In the geography project, the students made a map of different countries and their capitals.
Jane's project on endangered species won first place in the school competition.
The art project involved painting a beautiful landscape using watercolors.
The English project required the students to write a short story and present it to the class.
The students presented their science project on renewable energy sources.

senses.definition: a careful and systematic study of a subject to discover new facts or information about it

examples.x: Mark spent hours in the library doing research for his history paper.
The scientist conducted extensive research on the effects of climate change.
The professor presented the findings of her research at an international conference.
The research on social media usage revealed interesting trends among teenagers.
The team's research on consumer behavior guided their marketing strategy for the new product.

senses.definition: a brief account or statement, that gives only the main points of something, without mentioning the details
a piece of writing about a particular subject on a website, in a newspaper, magazine, or other publication

examples.x: I found an article online that explained how to improve time management skills.
I read an interesting article about healthy eating in a health magazine.
In the fashion magazine, there was an article about the latest trends.
The newspaper published an article about the local community event.
The science journal published an article on recent discoveries in space exploration.

senses.definition: one side or both sides of a sheet of paper in a newspaper, magazine, book, etc.

examples.x: I turned the page to continue reading the story in the book.
The children's book had beautiful illustrations on every page.
The magazine had colorful images on every page.
The teacher asked us to read a specific page from the history textbook.

senses.definition: a large board with a smooth white surface that we can write on, especially used for teaching or presentations

examples.x: During the language class, the teacher wrote new vocabulary on the whiteboard.
During the meeting, the presenter wrote important points on the whiteboard.
The office conference room is equipped with a large whiteboard for brainstorming sessions.
The teacher wiped the whiteboard clean after the lesson.

senses.definition: a restaurant, typically in colleges, hospitals, etc. where you choose and pay for your meal before carrying it to a table

examples.x: The cafeteria is a popular place for students to socialize during break time.
The cafeteria is open from 11:30 AM to 2:00 PM for lunch.
The cafeteria offers a variety of delicious sandwiches and salads.
The hospital cafeteria provides nutritious meals for patients and visitors.
We usually have lunch in the school cafeteria.

senses.definition: a series of written or printed names or items, typically one below the other

examples.x: John checked the list of ingredients before starting to cook.
The library had a list of recommended books for summer reading.
The magazine published a list of the top 10 restaurants in the city.
The teacher wrote the homework assignments on the board as a list.

senses.definition: a row of people or things behind each other or next to each other

examples.x: The buses were parked in a line at the bus station.
The children held hands and walked in a line on the field trip.
The soldiers marched in a disciplined line during the parade.
The students formed a line outside the classroom.
There was a long line of customers waiting to buy tickets.

senses.definition: a short piece of writing that helps us remember something

examples.x: I wrote a quick note to remind myself to buy groceries after work.
Jane left a sweet note on her friend's desk to brighten her day.
Mark jotted down some notes during the lecture to review later.
The meeting minutes were recorded in detailed notes.
The travel guide provided helpful notes for exploring the city's attractions.

senses.definition: to get the necessary grades in an exam, test, course, etc.

examples.x: Did you pass all your exams?
He hasn’t passed his driving test yet.
I'm not really expecting to pass first time.
She passed with flying colors.
I barely passed that test, it was so hard!

senses.definition: to be unsuccessful in an examination or course

examples.x: Despite studying hard, he failed the math test.
If you don't attend class regularly, you might fail the course.
John needs to improve his grades; otherwise, he will fail the semester.
Mark failed the history exam because he didn't study the material.

senses.definition: (of people) being somewhere particular

examples.x: During the roll call, the teacher marks those who are present.
Is the teacher present in the classroom?
John was present at the conference and actively participated in the discussions.
Sarah arrived late and missed the beginning of the presentation, but she is present now.
The manager is not present at the moment; she is in a meeting.

senses.definition: (of people) not present in a place

examples.x: Jane was absent from school yesterday due to a fever.
Please call the office if you're going to be absent from work today.
Sarah felt disappointed when her best friend was absent from her birthday party.
The boss noticed that some employees were absent from the morning meeting.

senses.definition: a student who is in the year before the last year of college or high school

examples.x: As juniors, they are preparing for standardized tests and college entrance exams.
Mark is excited to be a junior this year and looking forward to senior year activities.
Sarah's brother is a junior at the local high school, participating in sports and clubs.
The juniors are working on their final projects before becoming seniors.
The juniors organized a farewell party for the graduating seniors.

senses.definition: to pay full attention to someone or something specific

examples.x: Jane can focus better when she listens to instrumental music.
John finds it challenging to focus in a noisy environment.
She focused on studying for the upcoming exam.
The team leader focused on finding solutions to the problem.

senses.definition: a high-school or university student who is in their first year of education

examples.x: As a freshman, it's essential to seek guidance from seniors and professors for academic success.
Emily felt a mix of excitement and nervousness on her first day as a freshman.
John joined a club to get involved in campus life as a freshman.
Mark made new friends during his first week as a freshman in high school.
Sarah's brother is a freshman at the local university, studying computer science.

senses.definition: a student at a high school or university in their second year of education

examples.x: As a sophomore, I am taking more advanced classes in my major.
As a sophomore, it's essential to balance academics and extracurricular activities.
Her sister is a sophomore in high school and enjoys being part of the drama club.
Mark's brother is a sophomore in high school and has joined the debate team.
Sarah felt more confident as a sophomore and took on leadership roles.

senses.definition: a student who has one more year to graduate from a university or high school

examples.x: In college, seniors have the opportunity to complete a senior thesis.
The seniors are preparing for graduation and college.

senses.definition: a flat and hard tool made of wood, plastic, paper, etc. that is designed for specific purposes

examples.x: The teacher wrote the day's lesson on the board at the front of the classroom.
During the meeting, they brainstormed ideas and recorded them on the board for everyone to see.
The presenter used colorful markers to illustrate key points on the board during the seminar.
They installed a large board in the conference room for team collaboration and idea sharing.
She grabbed a whiteboard marker and began writing down ideas on the board during the meeting.

senses.definition: a picture that was made using pens, pencils, or crayons instead of paint

examples.x: The artist displayed a collection of stunning charcoal drawings at the gallery.
She won first place in the art competition for her beautifully detailed drawing of a flower.
The architect presented a detailed drawing of the building's floor plan to the client.
The art class learned various techniques for shading and blending in their charcoal drawings.
Drawing requires a good understanding of perspective and shading.
